BetterDiscord: How to Customize and Power Up Your Discord

Discord keeps getting bigger and bigger. A lot of companies have even swapped Slack out for it. But sometimes you want to customise your Discord more heavily so it feels nicer to use day-to-day, so this post is a quick walkthrough on optimising and customising it.
To customise Discord we need to install a tool called
. It lets you customise and tweak Discord pretty heavily, and the source is open on
if you're worried and want to read the code yourself.
The walkthrough below is all on macOS. Before you install, close the Discord app — otherwise you might hit weird issues.
After downloading, unzip it and you'll see a file called
You'll probably see "BetterDiscord.app can't be opened because the developer cannot be verified." macOS is pretty strict about unsigned apps.
Hit Cancel, then open System Preferences and search for "Security & Privacy".
Switch to the General tab and you'll see an "Open Anyway" option for BetterDiscord.app. Click it (remember to click the lock icon at the bottom left first so you can change settings).
It'll ask if you really want to open it — click Open.
Now you can install BetterDiscord. Just keep clicking Next, and when you reach the screen below, pick Discord.
Done — the extension is installed. Open Discord's User Settings, scroll the left sidebar all the way down, and you'll see a new section called "BETTERDISCORD".
Click into one you like and hit Download.
After downloading, you get a CSS file (
). Open Discord User Settings → Themes → Open Themes Folder.
Drop the CSS file you just downloaded into that folder.
Back in Discord, you'll see the theme show up in the list. Flip the toggle on and the theme applies.
Installing plugins works exactly the same way as themes, so I won't repeat the walkthrough — just head to the
I'll only list a few plugins I actually use. Installing too many plugins tends to make Discord slow to start, so be picky.
What it does: adds syntax highlighting to code snippets
What it does: shows when a user registered their Discord account. As an admin I sometimes need to filter out spam accounts, and this lets me verify how long an account has existed
What it does: lets you zoom in/out on images using a trackpad gesture
What it does: a really handy plugin for engineers — it underlines typos as you type
What it does: control Spotify from inside Discord. Nice if you don't want to keep switching windows
What it does: translates messages with Google Translate. If you're in international servers, this one's basically essential
What it does: double-click a message to enter edit mode directly
There are tons more plugins out there, but these are the ones I actually use.

Discord keeps blowing up — even companies are dropping Slack for it. But sometimes the default UI just isn't enough, and you want a more comfortable, customised setup. This post walks through using BetterDiscord to customise and enhance your Discord on macOS.
